title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 178.300 - Part-time schools established, when
text:
178.300.  Part-time schools established, when. — Whenever in any school district in this state not less than twenty-five employment certificates for children under sixteen years of age have been issued and are in full force and effect, the school district shall establish and maintain part-time schools, departments or classes for the employed children for not less than four hours per week and for a term not less than that in which schools are regularly in session in the district.
(L. 1963 p. 200 § 13-30) 
(Source: RSMo 1959 § 162.200) 
Child labor, 294.011 to 294.110 
Compulsory attendance of part-time schools, 167.051
